title: Auto-push branch and open PR after engineer agent commits
description:
Engineer agents commit work locally but never push the branch to GitHub. As a result:
- `github_pull_request` lookups return HTTP 404
- The work cannot be reviewed via GitHub
- Branch is lost if the workspace is wiped

The issue is visible in dashboard-0043: the branch `agent/engineer/dashboard-0043-as-a-user-i-want-to-be-able-to-see-the-dashboard` has commits locally but `gh api repos/MarcusGraetsch/rook-dashboard/commits` returns 404.

Objective:
- In the task-orchestrator skill (or the agent completion path in `task-dispatcher.mjs`), after the agent writes a terminal status with commits present: run `git push -u origin <branch>` and then `gh pr create` with the task title and handoff_notes as the PR body
- Write the resulting PR URL/number back to `canonical.github_pull_request`
- If push fails (e.g. no auth), log the error in `github_pull_request.last_error` but do not block task completion
- Scope: engineer and review agents only (researcher/coach don't commit code)

intake.brief: Push agent branches to GitHub and open PRs automatically after commits
intake.refinement_summary: Found during dashboard-0043 post-mortem: branch committed locally but never pushed.

handoff_notes:
Auto-push and PR creation implemented in task-dispatcher.mjs.

## What changed
- Added `runGh()` helper (analogous to `runGit()`) to invoke `gh` CLI with proper cwd handling
- Changed `gh pr create` invocations in `maybePushAndCreatePR()` from `runGit(repoPath, ['pr', 'create', ...])` to `runGh(repoPath, ['pr', 'create', '--base', 'main', ...])` — this was the core bug: `git pr create` is not a valid command, `gh` must be called directly
- `runGh()` runs `gh` with `cwd=repoPath` (no `-C` flag since gh operates relative to cwd like git)

## Root cause
The original code called `git pr create` via `runGit()` — git has no `pr` alias/subcommand, so `runGit` returned code 1 with "git: 'pr' is not a git command". The error was silently swallowed (non-blocking), so push succeeded but PR creation always silently failed.

## Validation
- Build: `npm run build` in rook-dashboard repo → exit code 0
- Push: `git push --force-with-lease -u origin agent/engineer/dashboard-0047-auto-push-pr-after-commit` → new branch pushed
- PR: `gh pr create --title "..." --body "..." --head agent/engineer/dashboard-0047-auto-push-pr-after-commit --base main` → https://github.com/MarcusGraetsch/rook-dashboard/pull/13
- PR metadata written back to canonical dashboard-0047.json (number=13, state=open)

plan.approach: Add a post-commit push+PR step to the agent completion path.
scope: Identify where in task-dispatcher.mjs or task-orchestrator the completion write happens
Add git push after terminal status write when commits[] is non-empty
Add gh pr create using task title + handoff_notes as body
Write PR number/url back to canonical github_pull_request
Non-blocking: failures go to last_error only

steps.title: Locate completion write path in dispatcher / task-orchestrator skill
Add git push step after commits present
Add gh pr create step with task title + handoff_notes body
Write PR metadata back to canonical task file

risks: gh CLI auth must be available in agent workspace — verify GITHUB_TOKEN is set in .env.d
Force-pushing to existing branches may overwrite in-flight review work — use --force-with-lease
plan.context: Dispatcher at /root/.openclaw/workspace/operations/bin/task-dispatcher.mjs. Task-orchestrator skill at /root/.openclaw/rook-agent/skills/task-orchestrator/. Branch pattern: agent/<agent>/<task-id>-<slug>

checklist.title: gh pr create with title + handoff body
